evidence: In 2025, we booked $1 billion of orders tied to data center applications.
We now expect to book approximately $3 billion of data center-related orders between 2025 and 2027
For the year ended December 31, 2025, we invested $600 million of R&D expense.
Cordant TM , a modular AI-enabled enterprise solution... designed to optimize assets, processes and energy use at scale
digital technologies such as advanced sensing diagnostics, data sciences, and AI
We are seeing growing demand for more intelligent operations and the adoption of AI-based solutions as part of our customers' digital transformations.
AI algorithms may be flawed or biased. Datasets used to train or develop AI systems may be insufficient, of inferior quality, or contain biased, incorrect or incomplete information.
Our AI governance review process and safeguards may not be adequate to protect against these risks and challenges.

emerging regulations or changes to intellectual property laws may limit or block the use of AI in our business or otherwise impose restrictions that may adversely affect the efficiency of our business processes

capex_mentions.quote: we booked $1 billion of orders tied to data center applications
capex_mentions.context: 2025 data center order bookings tied to power solutions

capex_mentions.quote: approximately $3 billion of data center-related orders between 2025 and 2027
we invested $600 million of R&D expense
capex_mentions.context: Total R&D spend for year ended December 31, 2025; includes digital/AI themes

limitations: The $600M R&D figure covers all R&D, not AI-specific spend; no dedicated AI capex line item is disclosed.
Data center order figures reflect revenue bookings (demand-side), not direct AI infrastructure investment by BKR itself.
ai_narrative_concreteness: Cordant and other named solutions are described as deployed but quantified business results (%, $, time saved) are absent, capping score at 4.
ai_investment_intensity: The multi-year $3B figure is an order-booking expectation, not a capex commitment; score reflects quantified scale but with caveat on nature of the figure.
